vocabulary.definition: a person or creature that is extremely large
vocabulary.example: A giant of a man was standing in the doorway.
vocabulary.pageHint: hut on the rock, door smashed

vocabulary.definition: wild, angry, or frightening in appearance
vocabulary.example: Harry looked up into the fierce, wild, shadowy face and saw that the beetle eyes were crinkled in a smile.

vocabulary.definition: long and rough, like unkempt hair or fur
vocabulary.example: His face was almost completely hidden by a long, shaggy mane of hair and a wild, tangled beard, but you could make out his eyes, glinting like black beetles under all the hair.

vocabulary.definition: to push or fit through a tight space
vocabulary.example: The giant squeezed his way into the hut, stooping so that his head just brushed the ceiling.
The Dursleys were cowering against the wall.

vocabulary.definition: to order someone not to do something
vocabulary.example: I forbid you to tell the boy anything!
vocabulary.pageHint: Uncle Vernon tries to stop Hagrid

vocabulary.definition: a person who can do magic
to speak with pauses and repeated sounds, often from nervousness
vocabulary.example: After a few minutes he stammered, “What does it mean, they await my owl?”

vocabulary.definition: thick paper used for important documents
vocabulary.example: and from yet another pocket inside his overcoat he pulled an owl—a real, live, rather ruffled looking owl—a long quill, and a roll of parchment.

vocabulary.definition: shocking behavior that upsets many people
to shake suddenly from fear or disgust
vocabulary.example: “Nah can’t spell it. All right—Voldemort.” Hagrid shuddered.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Turns eleven in a shack on a rock and finally learns the truth about his parents.

characters.name: Rubeus Hagrid
characters.description: Hogwarts gamekeeper who smashes down the door and tells Harry he is a wizard.

characters.name: Uncle Vernon
characters.description: Tries to stop Hagrid with a rifle and forbids anyone from telling Harry about magic.

characters.name: Aunt Petunia
characters.description: Bursts out with years of resentment about her witch sister Lily.

background.hook: On Harry's eleventh birthday, a giant smashes through the door of the Dursleys' hideaway. Over tea and sausages, Hagrid reveals that Harry is a wizard — and that the Dursleys have lied about how his parents died.
background.readingFocus: Watch how Harry reacts as each new truth lands. Notice what Hagrid reveals about Voldemort, Harry's scar, and the odd things Harry has done without knowing why.

options.text: order someone not to do it

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Hagrid arrives on Harry's birthday, tells him he is a wizard, and explains what happened to his parents
Harry shops for school supplies in Diagon Alley
The Dursleys move Harry back into the cupboard

comprehension.explanation: Hagrid breaks into the hut, reveals Harry's identity, delivers his Hogwarts letter, and tells him about Voldemort and his parents' deaths.

comprehension.stem: What does Hagrid bring Harry for his birthday?

options.text: A squashed chocolate cake with green icing
A new owl and a wand
A pile of gold coins from Gringotts
A set of black school robes

comprehension.explanation: Hagrid pulls a slightly squashed box from his coat with a sticky chocolate cake reading Happy Birthday Harry.

comprehension.stem: What does Hagrid do to Uncle Vernon's rifle?

options.text: Bends it into a knot and throws it in a corner
Loads it and fires at the ceiling
Uses it to prop open the door

comprehension.explanation: Hagrid jerks the gun from Vernon's hands, bends it like rubber, and tosses it away.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry react when Hagrid says he is a wizard?

options.text: He gasps in disbelief — "I'm a what?"
He cheers and dances around the hut
He already knew and is bored
He runs out into the storm

comprehension.explanation: There is silence in the hut, then Harry gasps, "I'm a what?"

comprehension.stem: What lie did the Dursleys tell Harry about his parents' deaths?

options.text: They died in a car crash
They died of a magical illness

comprehension.explanation: Harry says the Dursleys told him his parents died in a car crash; Hagrid roars that this is an outrage.

comprehension.stem: Why does Hagrid think Harry must be a wizard?

options.text: Strange things happen when Harry is scared or angry, like making Dudley's hair grow back
Harry already owns a wand and spell books
Harry can speak to snakes on command
Harry passed a magic exam at Stonewall High

comprehension.explanation: Hagrid asks if Harry never made things happen when upset — Harry remembers the haircut, the boa constrictor, and more.

comprehension.stem: What happens to Dudley at the end of the chapter?

options.text: Hagrid's umbrella gives him a curly pig's tail
He eats all of Hagrid's sausages
He reads Harry's Hogwarts letter aloud
He helps Hagrid fix the door

comprehension.explanation: After Vernon insults Dumbledore, Hagrid points his umbrella at Dudley and a pig's tail pokes through his trousers.

options.text: a person who can do magic

vocabulary.explanation: Hagrid tells Harry plainly, "Harry—yer a wizard."

vocabulary.stem: His face was almost completely hidden by a long, ___ mane of hair and a wild, tangled beard.
vocabulary.explanation: Hagrid's shaggy hair and beard almost hide his beetle-black eyes.
When Hagrid leaps up in anger, the Dursleys cower against the wall.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'fierce'?
vocabulary.explanation: Harry first sees Hagrid's fierce, wild, shadowy face before noticing his smile.

vocabulary.stem: After a few minutes he ___, "What does it mean, they await my owl?"
vocabulary.explanation: Harry is overwhelmed with questions and stammers when he finally speaks.

cloze.title: The Keeper of the Keys
paragraphs: On Harry's eleventh birthday, a (1) ___ of a man smashed through the door of the hut. His (2) ___ mane of hair and wild beard hid a (3) ___ face, but his beetle eyes crinkled in a smile.

When Hagrid learned the Dursleys had never told Harry about magic, he (4) ___ with rage and the family (5) ___ against the wall. Uncle Vernon tried to (6) ___ him from explaining, but Hagrid said Harry was a (7) ___.

gaps.explanation: A giant of a man stands in the doorway after the door is smashed off its hinges.
Hagrid's face is hidden by a long, shaggy mane of hair.
Harry looks up into Hagrid's fierce, wild, shadowy face.
Hagrid thunders "ALL WHAT?" when he learns how little Harry knows.
The Dursleys cower against the wall when Hagrid fills the hut with anger.
Uncle Vernon commands, "I forbid you to tell the boy anything!"
